rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is a central responsibility in improving a research draft to ensure the arguments are sound and coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Aligning claims with reported evidence is essential for enhancing the credibility of the draft.
rationale.R1.5: Reorganizing the structure at section and subsection levels is key to improving the narrative flow of the draft.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing the writing at sentence and paragraph levels is load-bearing for making the draft clearer and more professional.
rationale.RX.1: Disclosing uncertainties is central to improving the draft by promoting transparency in the research claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is crucial in refining the draft to maintain scientific integrity.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the scope ensures that improvements keep the draft focused on its intended topics.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding downstream harms is important when improving drafts in sensitive areas like AI to prevent misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring provenance and traceability supports the draft's reproducibility and ethical standards.
